Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 256. (no title) (CT 53 802)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

[x x x x] ⸢URU⸣.i-za-la ⸢x⸣+[x x x]

(1) [...... the to]wn of Izalla [......]

o 2'2'

[x x x x] ma-a ú-la-a tú [x x]

(2) [......] "Otherwise you [......]

o 3'3'

[x x ú-šag]-la-na-a-ši ù ⸢x⸣+[x x]

(3) [......] will deport us." [......]

o 4'4'

[x x x]+⸢x⸣ A-MEŠ ina IGI mad-bar ⸢x⸣+[x x]

(4) [......] water before the desert [......]

Bottom
b.e. 5'b.e. 5'

[x x x] ⸢LUGAL⸣ be-lí liš-⸢pur⸣ [x x x]

(5) [......] let the king my lord send [......]

Reverse
rr destroyed

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P314211/.
